description:
## Problem

Function origin is asked in three different ways across the compiler:

1. **prelude_fn_id_cutoff: u32** (LowerCtx, ir_lower.rs:228) — set after prelude fns are lowered. Consumers test \`fid.0 < cutoff\`.
2. **FnIr.category: FnCategory** — enum (User / Prelude / MultiClauseCont / LambdaLift / CpsCont / ControlFlowCont) set at lowering (fz-f88.5).
3. **String-prefix matching on f.name** — \`name.starts_with("k_")\`, \`"fn_clause_"\`, \`"lambda_"\`, \`"if_then"\`, etc.

(1) is a redundant micro-optimization. (3) is the larger problem: latent consumers asking origin questions through string matching, which silently breaks if naming conventions ever change.

## Discovery (already done — fz-f88 follow-up exploration)

**prelude_fn_id_cutoff consumers (2 sites):**
- src/ir_lower.rs:806–810 — \`build_source_info\` filters \`fid.0 < cutoff\` to exclude prelude Var spans (their byte offsets point into runtime.fz, not user source)
- src/ir_lower.rs:818–819 — same site, second use

**Name-prefix consumers (7 sites):**
- src/main.rs:710–712 — \`dump_specs_pipeline\` filters \`k_\`/\`fn_clause_\`/\`lambda_\` from the spec dump
- src/ir_inline.rs:1395 — filters \`if_then\`/\`if_else\`/\`if_join\` (control-flow continuations)
- src/ir_lower.rs:3372 — test helper, matches \`k_\` or \`lambda_\`
- src/ir_lower.rs:3649 — test helper, matches \`lambda_\`
- src/ir_lower.rs:3964 — test helper, matches \`k_\`
- src/ir_typer_tests.rs:1246 — test helper, finds \`lambda_\`
- src/ir_typer_tests.rs:1308 — test helper, finds \`k_\`

**Orthogonal / not-tangled:**
- \`boundary_fns\` (3 sites in ir_inline / ir_reducer) is \`@spec\`-driven, only ever populated for user fns. It answers "is this a reduction boundary?", not "where did this fn come from". No subsumption possible — leave it alone.
- \`extern_abi\` lives on both prelude and user fns; not used as an origin classifier today.

## Target shape

\`FnCategory\` is the single source of truth for fn origin. Every consumer asks a \`match\` on the variant it cares about. Migrations:

| Today                                   | After                                            |
| --------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ |
| \`fid.0 < cutoff\`                      | \`module.fn_by_id(fid).category == Prelude\`     |
| \`name.starts_with("k_")\`              | \`f.category == FnCategory::CpsCont\`            |
| \`name.starts_with("fn_clause_")\`      | \`f.category == FnCategory::MultiClauseCont\`    |
| \`name.starts_with("lambda_")\`         | \`f.category == FnCategory::LambdaLift\`         |
| \`name.starts_with("if_then")\` etc.    | \`f.category == FnCategory::ControlFlowCont\`    |
| filter prelude in spec dump             | \`f.category != FnCategory::Prelude\`            |

\`prelude_fn_id_cutoff\` is deleted after the build_source_info migration. The field, its constant maintenance in LowerCtx, and the constructor zero-init all go away together.

## Why this matters

- **Compile-time safety**: adding a new \`FnCategory\` variant becomes a compile error at every consumer that didn't update. String-prefix matching gives the same coverage zero help.
- **One question, one surface**: the cost of asking "is this prelude" today is "which of three representations am I supposed to use?" The answer should be exactly one.
- **Naming becomes presentational**: today \`name.starts_with("k_")\` is a load-bearing check disguised as a debug-formatting concern. After the migration, \`f.name\` is purely for diagnostics; the compiler doesn't depend on it.

## Scope shape (filed as one epic; child tickets sketched below)

Atoms that compose this work — each one-commit:

- **a)** Migrate ir_lower's build_source_info from \`fid.0 < cutoff\` to \`category == Prelude\`. Verify no behavior change in source-map output.
- **b)** Migrate ir_inline.rs:1395 control-flow-cont filter to \`category == ControlFlowCont\`.
- **c)** Migrate main.rs:710–712 (dump_specs name filter) to a category match.
- **d)** Migrate the 5 test-helper string-prefix matches to category matches.
- **e)** Delete \`prelude_fn_id_cutoff\` (field + assignment + zero-init).

After (e), \`f.name\` participates only in diagnostics. Verify with grep + cargo build.

## Out of scope

- Reordering or merging FnCategory variants (e.g. collapsing CpsCont + ControlFlowCont). They're semantically distinct at the point they're minted.
- \`boundary_fns\` — orthogonal, stays as-is.
- Anything in the codegen or runtime layer that doesn't currently ask origin questions. This is consolidation of existing consumers only.
title: Consolidate fn-origin data model — retire prelude_fn_id_cutoff + name-prefix matching, route everything through FnCategory
